sourceRef: tip:Three common Chapter 33 mistakes to avoid

slides.headline: Avoid These in Your Certifications
slides.body: Learn about the most frequent errors in Chapter 33 certifications and how to prevent them.

slides.headline: Incorrect Certification Dates
slides.body: Mistakenly certifying students who are no longer eligible for Chapter 33.
bullets: Incorrect certification dates on VA Form 22-1999
Certifying students past their eligibility period

slides.headline: Missing Eligibility Forms
slides.body: Not including necessary forms or documents in the certification package.
bullets: Missing VA Form 22-1999 in the certification package
Lack of necessary documentation for eligibility

slides.headline: Incorrect Eligibility Phrases
slides.body: Using outdated or incorrect eligibility phrases in the certification.
bullets: Using the wrong eligibility phrase in the certification document

slides.headline: Missing Verification
slides.body: Failing to verify the student's eligibility with the appropriate documentation.
bullets: Not verifying the student's eligibility with the correct documents
Failure to cross-check documents for accuracy

slides.headline: Lack of Review
slides.body: Not reviewing the certification package thoroughly before submission.
bullets: Lack of thorough review before submitting the certification
Failing to catch mistakes before submission

slides.body: Discover more tips and resources on avoiding these Chapter 33 mistakes. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Avoid these common Chapter 33 mistakes and learn how to catch them early. Get tips and resources to improve your certification process. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 5, 'Lack of Review', as it could benefit from a more compelling headline to draw more attention.
